leadText: Pickup (Japanese: ものひろい Pickup) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations III and IV 1.1.2 Generation V-VIII 1.1.3 Generation IX 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Items received 1.2.1.1 Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire, and Colosseum 1.2.1.2 Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen 1.2.1.3 Pokémon Emerald 1.2.1.3.1 Battle Pyramid 1.2.1.4 Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness 1.2.1.5 Pokémon Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum 1.2.1.6 Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver 1.2.1.7 Pokémon Black, White, Black 2, White 2, X, and Y 1.2.1.8 Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire 1.2.1.9 Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon 1.2.1.10 Pokémon Sword and Shield 1.2.1.11 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2.1.12 Pokémon Scarlet and Violet Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations III and IV [ edit | edit source ] Pickup has no effect during battle. Generation V-VIII [ edit | edit source ] At the end of the turn in which another Pokémon used a one-time-use item (including throwing an item with Fling or using Natural Gift ), a Pokémon with Pickup will collect the item if they are not holding anything. If more than one Pokémon has Pickup or Harvest , the faster Pokémon will restore an item first. If multiple items can be collected, the Pokémon will collect the item most recently consumed. A single Pokémon's Pickup will not activate more than once per turn. Pickup cannot collect an item if the Pokémon that consumed the item is no longer on the field at the end of the turn, or if the item has already been recovered (such as with Recycle , another Pokémon's Pickup, or Harvest). Pickup cannot collect an item that a Pokémon consumed before switching out, even if it switched back in again before the end of the turn. Pickup cannot collect items that have been destroyed with Incinerate , eaten with Bug Bite or Pluck , or knocked off with Knock Off . A popped Air Balloon cannot be collected. A Pokémon may only have one consumed item at a time; if a Pokémon consumes an item, then obtains another item and consumes it, the original consumed item cannot be collected, even if the second item is collected or restored then swapped away. If the Pokémon obtains another item after consuming an item, Pickup can still collect the old item as long as the Pokémon does not consume the new one. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] A Pokémon with Pickup recovers its consumed held item at the end of the turn in a battle against a wild Pokémon . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] This section is incomplete. Please feel free to edit this section to add missing information and complete it. Reason: in which games can pickup activate when you catch a Pokemon? After winning a battle, any Pokémon in the player's party that has this Ability and is not already holding an item has a 10% chance of creating a held item for itself, even if it did not participate in the battle and/or cannot battle . In Generation VIII , Pokémon with this Ability can find items even in the Battle Tower . Items received [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire, and Colosseum [ edit | edit source ] Rarity 30% 10% 5% 4% 1% Item Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen , the Ability focuses on Berries , which are not as readily available in Kanto and the Sevii Islands as they are in Hoenn . Rarity 15% 10% 5% 1% Item Pokémon Emerald [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Emerald , the Ability was changed to be affected by the Ability user's level. Level 30% 10% 4% 1% 1-10 11-20 21-30 31-40 41-50 51-60 61-70 71-80 81-90 91-100 Battle Pyramid [ edit | edit source ] In addition, the Battle Pyramid has a separate list of items that can be received; this table, however, is instead based on the number of floors/rounds the player has completed inside the Pyramid. The Pickup table and the item balls on the ground share this same pool of items. sections.title: In battle
sections.text: Contents 1 In battle 1.1 Generations III and IV 1.2 Generation V-VIII 1.3 Generation IX In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations III and IV [ edit | edit source ] Pickup has no effect during battle. Generation V-VIII [ edit | edit source ] At the end of the turn in which another Pokémon used a one-time-use item (including throwing an item with Fling or using Natural Gift ), a Pokémon with Pickup will collect the item if they are not holding anything. If more than one Pokémon has Pickup or Harvest , the faster Pokémon will restore an item first. If multiple items can be collected, the Pokémon will collect the item most recently consumed. A single Pokémon's Pickup will not activate more than once per turn. Pickup cannot collect an item if the Pokémon that consumed the item is no longer on the field at the end of the turn, or if the item has already been recovered (such as with Recycle , another Pokémon's Pickup, or Harvest). Pickup cannot collect an item that a Pokémon consumed before switching out, even if it switched back in again before the end of the turn. Pickup cannot collect items that have been destroyed with Incinerate , eaten with Bug Bite or Pluck , or knocked off with Knock Off . A popped Air Balloon cannot be collected. If the Pokémon obtains another item after consuming an item, Pickup can still collect the old item as long as the Pokémon does not consume the new one. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] A Pokémon with Pickup recovers its consumed held item at the end of the turn in a battle against a wild Pokémon .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Finds items if the Pokémon is not holding anything. MD T D S Enables the Pokémon to find items occasionally when it is not holding anything. B S L なにも どうぐを もってないと いつのまにか どうぐを ひろっている MD GtI The Pokémon may pick up an item when it goes to a different floor. * When the Pokémon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item! But if it is already holding an item, it won't pick up anything. * S MD When the Pokémon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item! But if it is already holding an item, it won't pick up anything. MD RT DX When the Pokémon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item. However, there will be places where there is nothing for the Pokémon to pick up.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Lillipup is the only Pokémon in the Unova Pokédex with the Pickup Ability. The majority of Pokémon that can have Pickup lose it upon evolving .
